title: Unwinding Irish Mulukhiyah Stew
description: A lazy-sunday Irish stew that blends grassy mulukhiyah with a fragrant infused olive oil, creating a gooey, comforting bowl that respects Ayurvedic tamasic principles and is cooked in a traditional donabe.
instructions: Heat the donabe over medium heat and add the infused olive oil.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Sprinkle in the ground coriander, cumin, and cayenne pepper, and stir for 1 minute.
Pour in the vegetable stock and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Add the mulukhiyah leaves, folding them into the broth, and cover the pot.
Simmer on low heat for 5 minutes until the leaves wilt and the stew thickens slightly.
Stir in the lemon juice and season with salt to taste, adjusting if needed.
Remove from heat and let the stew rest for 2 minutes before serving.
Garnish each bowl with chopped fresh parsley and serve hot.
